::: {#thm-chebyshev}
## Chebyshev's Inequality

For a random variable $X$ with mean $\mu$ and variance $\sigma^2$, for any $k > 0$:
$$P(|X - \mu| \geq k\sigma) \leq \frac{1}{k^2}$$
:::

::: {#proof}
Apply Markov's inequality to the non-negative random variable $(X - \mu)^2$.
:::

::: {#rem-chebyshev}
The bound is loose for small $k$; it becomes meaningful only once $k > 1$.
:::

::: {#sol-chebyshev}
Use $k = 2$ to conclude at least 75% of the mass lies within two standard deviations of the mean.
:::
<!-- Will work once https://github.com/quarto-dev/quarto-cli/pull/14312 is merged -->
<!-- The full argument appears in @sol-chebyshev, @rem-chebyshev.  -->
